# Quality Metrics and Validation Pass Rates Documentation

## Document Information
**Document Title:** Quality Metrics and Validation Pass Rates
**Protocol:** CDISCPilot01
**Date:** 2026-02-12
**Version:** 1.0

## 1. Executive Summary
This document provides a comprehensive overview of quality metrics from validation execution for the eSubmission Benchmark Package. The validation process included checks across multiple categories with an overall pass rate of 69%. Several issues were identified, primarily related to documentation mismatches rather than data integrity issues.

## 2. Validation Categories and Pass Rates

### 2.1 Overall Pass/Fail Rates by Category

| Category | Code Prefix | Passed | Failed | Total | Pass Rate |
|----------|-------------|--------|--------|-------|-----------|
| Data Checks | D-xxx | 0 | 5 | 5 | 0% |
| Log Checks | L-xxx | 0 | 0 | 0 | N/A |
| Programming Checks | P-xxx | 18 | 2 | 20 | 90% |
| Method Checks | M-xxx | 0 | 0 | 0 | N/A |
| **Total** | | **18** | **7** | **26** | **69%** |

### 2.2 Detailed Category Breakdown

#### 2.2.1 Data Checks (D-xxx)
Data checks focus on the consistency and accuracy of data values across datasets.

| Check ID | Description | Status | Severity |
|----------|-------------|--------|----------|
| D-002 | SDTM vs ADaM subject count | FAIL | Low |
| D-003 | USUBJID format | FAIL | Low |
| D-010 | Treatment arm values | FAIL | Medium |
| D-011 | Treatment N values | FAIL | Low |
| D-012 | ARM to TRT01P mapping | FAIL | Medium |

#### 2.2.2 Programming Checks (P-xxx)
Programming checks assess the correctness of programming logic and implementation.

| Check ID | Description | Status | Severity |
|----------|-------------|--------|----------|
| P-001 | Subject Traceability - SDTM to ADaM | PASS | N/A |
| P-002 | Subject Traceability - ADaM to TLF | PASS | N/A |
| P-003 | Population Consistency - ITT definition | PASS | N/A |
| P-004 | Analysis Data - Demographics values | PASS | N/A |
| P-005 | Analysis Data - Statistical calculations | PASS | N/A |
| P-006 | Variable Lineage - AGE | PASS | N/A |
| P-007 | Variable Lineage - SEX | PASS | N/A |
| P-008 | Variable Lineage - RACE | PASS | N/A |
| P-009 | Variable Lineage - TRT01P | PASS | N/A |
| P-010 | Variable Lineage - Primary endpoint | FAIL | Medium |
| P-011 | Variable Lineage - Derived variables | PASS | N/A |
| P-012 | Variable Lineage - BASE calculation | PASS | N/A |
| P-013 | Variable Lineage - CHG calculation | FAIL | Medium |
| P-014 | Programming Standards - CDISC compliance | PASS | N/A |
| P-015 | Programming Standards - Code structure | PASS | N/A |
| P-016 | Programming Standards - Documentation | PASS | N/A |
| P-017 | Programming Standards - Error handling | PASS | N/A |
| P-018 | Programming Standards - Output format | PASS | N/A |
| P-019 | Programming Standards - Metadata | PASS | N/A |
| P-020 | Programming Standards - Version control | PASS | N/A |

## 3. Severity Classification

### 3.1 Severity Definitions
- **High:** Critical issues that prevent submission or significantly impact data integrity
- **Medium:** Issues that may impact interpretation or require documentation updates
- **Low:** Minor issues that do not impact data integrity but should be addressed

### 3.2 Severity Distribution

| Severity | Count | Percentage |
|----------|-------|------------|
| High | 0 | 0% |
| Medium | 4 | 57% |
| Low | 3 | 43% |
| **Total** | **7** | **100%** |

## 4. Outstanding Issues

### 4.1 High Severity Issues
None identified.

### 4.2 Medium Severity Issues

| Issue ID | Description | Category | Status | Resolution |
|----------|-------------|----------|--------|------------|
| I-001 | Documentation vs Data Mismatch - Treatment coding | D-010 | Open | Update Protocol/SAP to use actual treatment coding |
| I-002 | ARM to TRT01P mapping inconsistency | D-012 | Open | Create mapping table |
| I-003 | Variable Lineage - Primary endpoint | P-010 | Open | Verify derivation logic |
| I-004 | Variable Lineage - CHG calculation | P-013 | Open | Review calculation method |

### 4.3 Low Severity Issues

| Issue ID | Description | Category | Status | Resolution |
|----------|-------------|----------|--------|------------|
| I-005 | Subject Count Difference - SDTM vs ADaM | D-002 | Open | Document expected behavior |
| I-006 | USUBJID Format Documentation | D-003 | Open | Update documentation |
| I-007 | Treatment N values - DM includes screen failures | D-011 | Open | Filter to ITT only |

## 5. Validation Pass Rate Analysis

### 5.1 Pass Rate by Category
- **Programming Checks:** 90% (18/20)
- **Data Checks:** 0% (0/5)
- **Overall:** 69% (18/26)

### 5.2 Pass Rate Trend
The validation results show strong performance in programming logic and implementation (90% pass rate) but highlight documentation issues in the data checks category. The overall pass rate of 69% indicates that while the core programming is sound, there are documentation and data definition issues that need to be addressed.

## 6. Recommendations

### 6.1 Immediate Actions
1. Update Protocol/SAP documentation to reflect actual treatment coding conventions
2. Create a mapping table between ARM and TRT01P variables
3. Update USUBJID format documentation
4. Document the expected subject count difference between SDTM and ADaM

### 6.2 Follow-up Actions
1. Verify primary endpoint derivation logic
2. Review CHG calculation method
3. Implement filtering to ITT population for treatment N value comparisons

## 7. Conclusion
The validation execution identified 7 issues across data and programming checks, with no high severity issues. The majority of issues (57%) are classified as medium severity, primarily related to documentation mismatches. The programming checks demonstrated a strong 90% pass rate, indicating robust programming logic and implementation. With the recommended actions, the package is expected to achieve full compliance.
